pr-evidence/standalone-benchmark-evidenc.gpt55_review_summary: GPT-5.5 review initially found that the benchmark folder contract was too weak and that release evidence could still point at non-standalone paths. The PR added a strict benchmark-folder allowlist, status-sidecar validation, committed artifact path checks, and size/hash verification; the reviewer rechecked the fixes and approved.
verification: GPT-5.5 review found weak artifact-contract and non-standalone release-path issues; both were fixed before approval
what_changed: Added standalone human-readable Databricks benchmark evidence folders under benchmarks/databricks for the strict g6/L4 benchmark, g5/A10G compatibility benchmark, storage-reader benchmark, and native engine probe evidence.

Added governance coverage that allowlists the benchmark folder contents, validates Databricks run-status sidecars, checks benchmark summaries, and verifies release-evidence artifact paths and hashes point at benchmarks/.
Linked the benchmark folders from the README, docs README, and V1 requirements matrix while keeping raw pr-evidence and ignored databricks-runs output separate.
pr-evidence/standalone-benchmark-evidenc.why: Benchmark and release reviewers need standalone human-readable benchmark folders instead of having to inspect raw PR evidence or temporary Databricks run output.
